George Freeman is a prominent UK politician, UK Trade Envoy, former UK Minister for Science who led programs for Life Science, Agritech, Biotechnology, Space, Quantum, and Fusion.

2025 ANNUAL REPORT BACK

As your local constituency MP, I take seriously the responsibility of trying to represent everyone in Mid Norfolk and to report back on what I’m doing. 

Each year I send an Annual Report to all those constituents who have emailed me. 

You can read my Annual Report email here or pdf version here.

Yours, 

George.


Science & Technology
Science, Innovation and Technology Committee: Science Diplomacy
27 January 2026

George Freeman questions Will Whitehorn, Chair, Seraphim Space Investment Trust about space investment.
Science, Innovation and Technology Committee: Science Diplomacy
27 January 2026

George Freeman MP questions Dr Alice Bunn, President, UKspace and Graham Turnock, Ex-CEO, UK Space Agency on OneWeb.
Science, Innovation and Technology Committee: Science Diplomacy
27 January 2026

George Freeman MP questions Dr Alice Bunn, President, UKspace and Graham Turnock, Ex-CEO, UK Space Agency on the space sector in the UK.
All Science & Technology

Linkedin

Parliament
Image
Science, Innovation and Technology Committee
22 April 2026

George Freeman questions Professor Sir Leszek Borysiewicz, Government's preferred candidate for the role of chair, UK Research and Innovation (UKRI).
Image
George Freeman calls for an end to the Government’s policy of massive subsidised solar “farm” power stations on agricultural land
14 April 2026

George Freeman calls for an end to the Government’s policy of massive subsidised solar “farm” power stations on agricultural land and switching to solar panels on rooftops, roads, motorway sidings, reservoirs & industrial sites instead of farmland. 
Image
George Freeman highlights the scale of flooding and impact on insurers
18 March 2026

George Freeman intervenes in a debate on the impact of flooding in rural communities to highlight the shear scale of the insurance problem risking serious economic damage to our economy and, as such, is an issue of national importance.
All Speeches in the House

X


News
A Healthcare Plan for Dereham
28 April 2026

Towns like Dereham cannot keep having new homes built without additional investment in local facilities, services and infrastructure, especially health and care services.
Image
George Freeman calls for an end to the Government’s policy of massive subsidised solar “farm” power stations on agricultural land
14 April 2026

George Freeman calls for an end to the Government’s policy of massive subsidised solar “farm” power stations on agricultural land and switching to solar panels on rooftops, roads, motorway sidings, reservoirs & industrial sites instead of farmland.  
George Freeman fights for rural communities facing heating oil price hikes
11 March 2026

Fuel suppliers in Norfolk are cancelling orders for domestic heating oil in response to the Middle East crisis, raising concerns that rural households could be left without fuel.
All News

George Freeman MP
Rooms 333-335
Breckland Business Centre
St Withburga Lane
Dereham
Norfolk, NR19 1FD

Telephone:
01953 600617

email:
george.freeman.mp@parliament.uk


Breckland District: All Saints, Buckenham, Burgh and Haverscroft, Dereham Central, Dereham Humbletoft, Dereham Neatherd, Dereham Toftwood, Eynsford, Haggard De Toni, Hermitage, Launditch, Necton, Queen's, Shipdham, Springvale and Scarning, Swanton Morley, Taverner, Templar, Two Rivers, Upper Wensum, Upper Yare, Watton, and Wissey; South Norfolk District: Abbey, Cromwells, Hingham and Deopham, Northfields, Rustens, Town, and Wicklewood.
